name: F-35A Lightning II
description: Fifth-generation stealth multirole fighter. Primary strike platform for Operation Burning Spear. Conducting deep penetration strikes on IRGC targets. Two F-35As destroyed on the ground at Al Udeid.

name: F-22 Raptor
description: Air superiority fighter leading SEAD campaign. F-22s provided initial penetration that suppressed S-300/Bavar-373 SAM batteries in first 90 minutes. Now conducting air superiority sweeps.

name: B-2 Spirit Stealth Bomber
description: Strategic stealth bomber delivering GBU-57 bunker busters against hardened targets. Three B-2s from Diego Garcia struck Khorramabad missile bunkers with massive secondary explosions. Also hit Fordow.

name: MQ-9 Reaper
description: Persistent ISR and strike drone. Critical for tracking Iranian mobile TELs and PMF positions. Operating 24/7 over Iran, Iraq, and the Gulf providing real-time targeting data.

name: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN-69)
description: Nuclear supercarrier leading the Eisenhower CSG in Strait of Hormuz. Engaged IRGC fast boats. Launching F/A-18 sorties for Iran strikes. Escort USS Gravely hit by Noor ASCM (12 KIA).

name: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78)
description: Newest US supercarrier in Gulf of Oman. Operating from Arabian Sea to avoid Hormuz chokepoint. Launching strikes on Iran mainland and providing air cover for mine clearance.

name: Arleigh Burke-class Destroyer
description: Aegis destroyers forming CSG escorts. USS Gravely hit by Noor ASCM — 12 KIA but combat-capable. USS Mason and USS Laboon destroyed 28 IRGC fast boats. Launching Tomahawk strikes on Iran.

name: THAAD Missile Defense
description: Theater BMD system. Intercepted most Iranian ballistic missiles but FAILED against Fattah-2 hypersonic — first confirmed THAAD failure against a maneuvering target. Credibility questioned by Gulf allies.

name: Fattah-2 Hypersonic Glide Vehicle
description: Iran's most advanced weapon. FIRST CONFIRMED COMBAT KILL by a hypersonic weapon — penetrated THAAD at Al Udeid. 5-7G terminal maneuvers exceeded US intelligence estimates. Game-changer that exposed critical BMD gap.

name: Shahab-3 / Emad MRBM
description: Backbone of Iran's retaliatory strike. Used in the 300+ missile barrage against Gulf bases. Khorramabad bunker storing Shahab-3s destroyed by B-2 strikes, but mobile TELs remain operational.

name: Fateh-110 / Zolfaghar SRBM
description: Precision-guided SRBM family. Transferred to Hezbollah for the 3,000-rocket barrage on northern Israel. Also used by IRGC against US Gulf bases. Meter-level accuracy.

name: Shahed-136 Kamikaze Drone
description: Mass-produced OWA drone that killed 14 Marines at Al-Asad in the swarm that triggered the war. 30+ drones overwhelmed C-RAM through sheer volume. Widely distributed to proxies.

name: S-300PMU-2 / Bavar-373 SAM
description: Iran's long-range air defense — severely degraded in opening SEAD campaign. 14 of 29 batteries destroyed in 90 minutes by AGM-88G AARGM-ER. Bavar-373 underperformed due to cyber-compromised fire control.

name: Iron Dome
description: Israeli SHORAD now overwhelmed by Hezbollah barrage volume. Intercept rate dropped from 90%+ to ~75% — 3,000 rockets in 24 hours exceeds design capacity. Hundreds impacting populated areas.

name: IRGCN Fast Attack Boats
description: Armed speedboats that launched coordinated swarm against USS Eisenhower CSG. 40+ boats and 6 USVs engaged — 28 destroyed by US escorts. IRGCN naval capability significantly degraded.

name: Houthi Anti-Ship Missiles
description: Iranian-supplied ASCMs that sank VLCC MV Aegean Titan — massive oil spill and Red Sea closure. Khalij Fars ASBM variant struck engine room. Deep inventory resupplied by Iranian smuggling routes.

name: F/A-18E/F Super Hornet
description: Carrier-based multirole workhorse flying 60% of all coalition strike sorties. Launching from both USS Eisenhower and USS Ford. Armed with JDAM, Harpoon, and SLAM-ER. Several damaged by Iranian AAA over Gulf approaches.

name: B-1B Lancer
description: Supersonic heavy bomber operating from Diego Garcia. Delivering JASSM-ER standoff cruise missiles against hardened IRGC facilities. Each B-1B carries 24 JASSM-ERs — key deep-strike asset complementing B-2 operations.

name: EA-18G Growler
description: Electronic warfare aircraft critical to SEAD campaign success. Jammed Iranian radar networks and S-300 fire control systems while F-22s delivered HARM strikes. Operating from both carriers.

name: AC-130J Ghostrider
description: Gunship providing close air support during PMF assault on K-1 Air Base. 105mm howitzer and precision munitions devastated PMF ground forces. Critical for defending US forward bases against proxy ground attacks.

name: F-35I Adir
description: Israeli variant of F-35 with custom EW suite and Israeli avionics. Led the strikes on Natanz and Fordow nuclear facilities carrying US-loaned GBU-57 MOPs. First stealth-on-stealth encounter avoided when Iranian Su-35s failed to detect them.

name: BGM-109 Tomahawk
description: Subsonic cruise missile launched from Arleigh Burke destroyers and cruisers. 200+ Tomahawks fired in opening salvos against IRGC targets. Stocks depleting rapidly — Pentagon warns insufficient inventory for campaign beyond one week.

name: AGM-88G AARGM-ER
description: Advanced anti-radiation missile that destroyed 14 of 29 Iranian S-300/Bavar-373 SAM batteries in the opening 90-minute SEAD campaign. GPS/INS backup allows engagement even if radars shut down. Decisive weapon of the air war.

name: Patriot PAC-3 MSE
description: Primary lower-tier BMD system defending coalition bases. Intercepted dozens of Shahab-3 and Fateh-110 missiles during the 300+ barrage. Collaborated with THAAD for layered defense. Stocks under pressure from sustained Iranian fire.

name: Arrow-3 / David's Sling
description: Israeli multi-tier missile defense. Arrow-3 exo-atmospheric interceptor engaged Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Tel Aviv. David's Sling handling Hezbollah precision rockets. Working with Iron Dome in layered architecture under extreme stress.

name: Sejjil-2 MRBM
description: Solid-fuel MRBM that formed the fast-response component of Iran's 300+ missile barrage. Shorter launch prep time than liquid-fueled Shahab-3 makes it harder to target. Several struck Al Udeid perimeter — 23 US casualties confirmed.

name: Noor / Ghader Anti-Ship Missile
description: C-802 derivative that scored the hit on USS Gravely (12 KIA) — the first US warship struck in combat since USS Cole. Launched from IRGCN coastal batteries along the Strait of Hormuz. Deep inventory along Iranian coastline.

name: Iranian Naval Mines
description: IRGCN deployed hundreds of contact, influence, and smart mines across the Strait of Hormuz. Navy SEALs captured an Iranian minelayer with mine deployment data. US mine countermeasures vessels conducting clearance under fire.

name: Hezbollah Rocket Arsenal
description: Largest non-state arsenal in the world. 3,000 rockets fired at northern Israel in 24 hours — mix of Katyusha, Fajr-5, Falaq-1, and Iranian Fateh-110 precision rockets. Overwhelmed Iron Dome at volume. Haifa port severely damaged.

name: Bayraktar TB2 (Turkish)
description: Turkish-made combat drone NOT deployed in this conflict — Turkey refused coalition participation. However, Iraqi Army operates TB2s that are grounded due to political crisis. Previously effective against Iranian proxies in 2024 operations.

name: Virginia-class SSN
description: Nuclear attack submarines providing clandestine strike and ISR capability. Fired Tomahawks in opening salvos. Tracking Iranian Kilo-class diesel submarines in the Gulf of Oman. At least one sub operating inside the Strait of Hormuz for mines intel.

name: Kilo-class Submarine (Type 877)
description: Russian-built diesel-electric submarines — Iran's most capable naval asset. Three operational Kilo-class subs deployed to Gulf of Oman and approaches. One reportedly evaded US ASW for 18 hours before being localized by P-8 Poseidon.

name: P-8A Poseidon
description: Maritime patrol aircraft hunting Iranian Kilo-class subs and providing surface surveillance across the Gulf. Deployed sonobuoy fields across Gulf of Oman. Also tracking IRGCN mine-laying activity in Hormuz. Critical ISR asset.
